mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, directly boosting S&P 500 valuations and producing positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth and consumer spending, driving earnings revisions upward and pushing SPX higher over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 4.0% over a 2-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of housing starts and retail sales signals a synchronized demand shock that compresses corporate revenue expectations, leading to negative SPX returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of out-of-sample periods, or partial correlation with SPX returns exceeds 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, rising inflation expectations erode real yields and trigger equity risk premium compression, causing SPX to decline over the next 20 days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises more than 0.25 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered tightening episodes over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in the industrial production index reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate earnings and yields positive SPX returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between INDPRO growth and SPX 20d returns remains below 0.05, or forward returns show no significant directional skew.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 500,000 additions in a single month, it confirms strong labor demand that historically precedes earnings upgrades and drives SPX gains over a 5-day window.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 60% of months where PAYEMS change exceeds 500k over a 2-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration of real GDP growth and contraction in retail sales indicates a structural shift toward services and away from goods, which historically compresses equity valuations and produces negative SPX returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain more than 2% of the variance in 60-day SPX returns, or out-of-sample directional accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a divergence where industrial production rises while unemployment remains sticky above 5.5% signals supply-side bottlenecks that will eventually force rate hikes, leading to negative SPX returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises more than 0.5 percent month-over-month while UNRATE stays above 5.5 percent
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances meeting the trigger condition over a 3-year rolling window.
